_comment: question-insert sites that do NOT set draft_status. Each entry MUST have reason + resolves_when {kind,ref,expected_by}. NULL≡active is the legal semantics for these writers (the question IS an active pool item). teaching-check also sets draft_status explicitly post-YUK-350-L2 — kept here as harmless-redundant (chain-merge guard); the audit silently passes an allowlisted-AND-explicit file. (YUK-358 决定3: the embedded_check_generate writer was deleted with the orphan chain.) See docs/design/2026-05-15-data-assumptions.md.

src/capabilities/ingestion/server/auto-e.reason: Auto-enrolled questions are active pool items by design — NULL≡active is correct; they enter the review pool immediately.

resolves_when.ref: permanent: auto-enroll questions are intentionally active (NULL≡active); revisit only if a draft-then-promote flow is introduced for auto-enroll

src/capabilities/ingestion/api/import.ts.reason: Imported questions are user-provided active pool items — NULL≡active is correct.

resolves_when.ref: permanent: imported questions are intentionally active (NULL≡active)

src/capabilities/ingestion/api/mistakes..reason: Mistake-recorded questions are active pool items (the user got them wrong; they belong in review) — NULL≡active is correct.

resolves_when.ref: permanent: mistake questions are intentionally active (NULL≡active)

src/server/questions/parts.ts.reason: Paper/quiz part questions are active pool items written as part of an assembled paper — NULL≡active is correct.

resolves_when.ref: permanent: paper part questions are intentionally active (NULL≡active)

src/capabilities/copilot/server/teaching.reason: Container-only teaching checks now set draft_status='draft' explicitly (YUK-350 L2). Kept here as a harmless-redundant chain-merge guard; the audit silently passes an allowlisted-AND-explicit file.

resolves_when.ref: redundant post-L2 (file sets draft_status explicitly); remove this entry once W0 is merged — kept only so the L2->L4 chain never hard-fails on merge order
